Patch Set 3:

Do you have any performance numbers to compare this implementation vs. the current one?

IIRC, Liang compared atomic vs. spinlock in the past.  It does appear that atomic operations were less expensive under contention than a single global spinlock.  However, spinlock only blocks CPUs that are accessing that lock, while atomics are executed with bus-locked cycles, so in the (presumably?) more common case where this lock is not contended I'd think it has less impact?

Liang?

Patch Set 3:

I agree with Andreas that this patch will increase overhead of common case,   

 1. request buffer is not going to increase forever, so we probably should not care too much about performance of high contention case which is rare, especially with the cost of increasing overhead in most common case
 2. even it is in high contention case, I still suspect this patch can do better, because all threads in a partition will need to change the same atomic for twice to find out a race condition, which is not so good. In current implementation, most likely, threads only need to test cached value.
 3. spinning at here is still because of vmap_area_lock of LInux kernel, so the best we can do is keep most threads away from this path, current implementation can already do this for us.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Liang, so I think you mean -1 on this patch (you didn't specify), and leave the current implementation?  I'm marking -1 based on this and my own expectations that this is more overhead than the existing approach.

Patch Set 3: Abandoned

not accepted

parents.subject: LU-2791 ldlm: release reference against failed lock

commit.subject: LU-2432 ptlrpc: alloc_rqbd spin on vmap_area_lock
commit.message:
LU-2432 ptlrpc: alloc_rqbd spin on vmap_area_lock

Other version of implementation with following pros:
* Code simplification.
* Better readability.
* Better behavior (without waiting on lock) in case of
  high contention.

Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Idcfe3702040771771a894a565c0aa7a5286075a6
